element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Resource management - must eat to maintain power meter
Avoidance - dog barks are lethal; power meter depletes when threatened
Resource depletion timer - depletes faster in Japanese version (Wan-Wan Panic) than US version

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Resource-management driven tension: player must balance exploration for food against threat of dog encounters while managing continuous power meter drain

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to hunger/power meter mechanic and basic dog threat avoidance
notable_spikes: Power meter depletion rate differs between regions - Japanese version (Wan-Wan Panic) depletes much faster, creating higher difficulty

notable_level_patterns.description: Core gameplay loop: cherub must locate and consume food while avoiding dog encounters. Power meter serves as time pressure mechanic forcing constant movement and decision-making

regional_variance.note: Significant mechanical difference: power meter depletes faster in Japanese version (Obake no Q-tarou: Wanwan Panic), making it substantially more difficult than US release
regional_variance.visual_changes: Title screen darker in US version; character sprites redrawn; UI elements changed (Q logo to hearts)

extraction_notes: Source material is limited (stub article status). Game mechanics and level design details not documented in available source. Spatial patterns, detailed difficulty curve, and level sequencing could not be extracted due to lack of gameplay documentation.
